title: Senior AI Engineer
company_focus:
Mobile industry association developing AI applications and
systems for telecommunications networks
description:
Department: Technical

Team: Networks

Location: London, Hybrid (remote considered in exceptional
circumstances)

Position type: Permanent

What the hiring manager says

You are not expected to be an AI expert, but have a strong
interest in transitioning to the role in a senior capacity.

You will be working for a world class AI applications and
systems team. This is a new team with the opportunity to
play a foundational role. We are moving from a successful
proof-of-concept to product prototype phase. You will have
the opportunity to learn new techniques and algorithms at
the cutting edge of Natural Language Processing (NLP) and
Large Language Model limitations (LLM).

Dr G.A.McHale, Technical Director, AI & Data Science

About the Team

The team is led by someone with more than 25 years AI
experience in bio-inspired architectures, reinforcement
learning, expert systems, scheduling, meta-heuristics,
robotics and natural language processing (including LLMs).
We have recruited an experienced scientific computing
developer with a strong mathematics background in
theoretical physics, whose responsibilities relate to
distributed systems and the GPU optimization of AI
algorithms. We expect the team to grow in size considerably
over the next few years and are currently looking for
someone with complementary expertise as a senior member of
the team.

About the Role

Key accountabilities include :

These are quite broad based but include initially the
optimization of existing algorithms, third-party technology
evaluation integration, together with more general
programming roles. At this stage we are a small team where
everyone is expected to share the responsibility for less
glamorous programming tasks! Over time it would be expected
that you assume a more research-oriented role, with the
ability to devise novel solutions to challenging problems.
We have an existing code base, so you will be expected to
assume some ownership and responsibility for existing code,
with a view to maintenance and enhancement.

About You

- You possess an academic background in mathematics, physics
  or engineering and have a passion for programming
  (desirable).
- Academic skills should include literature search,
  mathematical fluency, critical thinking and inventiveness
  with regards to designing algorithm and model
  architectures.
- You have proven programming skills, including implementing
  complex algorithms, testing, verification and may have
  written code for accessibility tools or productivity
  tools.
- Although not essential it would be valuable if the candidate
  had some exposure to the user of development of LLMs Any
  telecommunications related expertise would be a bonus but
  is not mandatory.
- Any knowledge of AI techniques related to NLP, Reinforcement
  Learning, or Agent-based-modelling are desirable, but are
  not essential.
- Candidates that have followed a non-conventional career
  paths are encouraged to apply, however high numeracy
  skills and demonstrable programming experience is
  essential.
- It is desirable that you have (at a minimum) a Master’s
  degree or Doctorate qualification.
- You demonstrate effective team skills including being
  adaptable and collaborative.

About your skills

Key skills for this role would include Teamwork, Commercial
Awareness, Innovation, Research and Analysis, along with
some key technical skills encompassing Mathematical fluency,
Programming and Engineering.

We strive to offer a meaningful and inclusive application
experience for all candidates. Should you require any
accommodations or adjustments due to a disability or for any
other reason during the hiring process, please contact
talent@gsma.com with your request.

Contract type

Regular

Worker type

Employee

What We Offer

Working at the GSMA offers you unparalleled access to the
mobile industry. We offer a chance to truly shape the
direction of mobile, whatever your role. By joining the
GSMA, you will be exposed to a fast-paced rapidly evolving
environment, working on global solutions, genuinely
fascinating and industry-changing projects and a stimulating
and dynamic environment designed to enable you to flourish.

In addition to architect-designed offices and competitive
compensation, our benefits include fantastic learning &
development opportunities, generous holiday allowances, four
additional days off for professional development and many
others.

To learn more about the GSMA, visit our career site, our
LinkedIn page and our Twitter page.

Being You at the GSMA

We care deeply about diversity, equity and inclusivity and
aspire to be the best at it. Your well-being and work/life
balance is important, so flexi-time and remote working is
available to all staff. We're keen to ensure everyone is
equal, represented and connected so we particularly
encourage applications from all demographics. The sucess of
the GSMA year on year will continue to be contributed by
people from all walks of life.

GSMA Values

Our values not only drive our culture – they shape how we
work and interact inside and outside our global
organisation.

Passionately driven

We approach everything we do with unparalleled capability,
tenacity and commitment, knowing that the challenging scale,
pace and complexity of our work is what leads to its world-
changing impact.

Insightful leaders

We continually develop and engage our expertise, insight and
creativity so that we’re always ready to respond to the
changing landscape with authority, agility and nuance.

Stronger together

We lean on each other so the industry can lean on us,
embracing our diversity by actively seeking out perspectives
and skill sets beyond our own, fuelling each other’s
successes and constantly asking how we can help.

Underpinning our values is our collective mindset to show up
purposefully as good human beings every day, in every
situation. When we’re at our best – we are collaborative,
considerate and compassionate to others, and we create a
safe space for one another to thrive, assuming positive
intent in our colleagues. And if we aren’t at our best and
the pressure is on – we feel free to be ourselves but still
remain curious, lean into the tough stuff and we are always
respectful to others and accountable for the part we play.
responsibilities: Optimize existing AI algorithms and integrate third-party technologies

Devise novel solutions to challenging problems in a research-oriented role
Share responsibility for general programming tasks within a small team
Learn and apply cutting-edge NLP and LLM techniques and limitations
use_cases: Natural Language Processing applications and systems
Large Language Model applications with focus on limitations and optimization
Reinforcement learning and agent-based modeling solutions
AI applications for telecommunications and mobile industry networks
Bio-inspired AI architectures and expert systems
ai_classification_reasoning:
The role involves working directly with AI algorithms
including LLMs, NLP, and reinforcement learning. Key
responsibilities include optimizing existing AI algorithms,
third-party technology integration, and GPU optimization of
AI systems, with hands-on implementation of complex
algorithms.
